name: PowerShell Script Execution

parameters.command: powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "Get-Process | Select-Object Name,Id,CPU | Sort-Object CPU -Descending | Select-Object -First 10"

expectedSignals: PowerShell Script with Token Impersonation Capabilities

name: Windows Command Shell Execution
expectedSignals: Unusual Process Execution via Windows Command Prompt
System Information Discovery via Windows Built-In Commands

name: Unix Shell Execution
expectedSignals: System Information Discovery via Unix Commands

name: Scheduled Task Creation and Deletion
expectedSignals: Scheduled Task Created by Unusual Process
Windows Scheduled Task Created or Modified by Non-system User

name: Registry Run Key Persistence

parameters.command: reg.exe add "HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run" /v EmulationTest /t REG_SZ /d calc.exe /f && reg.exe delete "HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run" /v EmulationTest /f

expectedSignals: Persistence via Run Key with Suspicious Executable
Registry Run Key with Unusual Executable
Startup Persistence by a Suspicious Process

name: Process Discovery via Built-in Tool
expectedSignals: Enumeration of Privileged Local Groups Membership

name: LSASS Process Information Access

parameters.command: tasklist.exe /fi "imagename eq lsass.exe" /v && powershell.exe -NoProfile -NonInteractive -Command "Get-Process lsass | Select-Object Id,Name,WorkingSet,SessionId"

expectedSignals: LSASS Memory Access from Unknown Executable
Potential Credential Access via LSASS Memory

name: File Deletion for Indicator Removal
expectedSignals: Suspicious File Deletion by Endpoint Security

name: Web Protocol C2 Simulation
expectedSignals: Potential Command and Control via Internet Explorer

name: Registry Modification

parameters.command: reg.exe add "HKCU\Software\EmulationTest" /v TestValue /t REG_SZ /d "EmulationData" /f && reg.exe delete "HKCU\Software\EmulationTest" /f

expectedSignals: Windows Registry File Creation in SMB Share
